summary: Start M7 UX recovery validation after task rail collapsed drawers closed on origin/main. Validate first-screen/map/decision/task-rail player experience before content lock can be reconsidered.
acceptance_criteria: Add or update product-level UX smoke tests verifying the first screen exposes identity, situation, objective, recommended action, map legend, and no player-mode debug labels.
Validate that the default map no longer presents as a rectangular grid in player mode and that route semantics are explained before content lock reconsideration.
Record screenshot evidence for desktop 1440x900 and at least one supported smaller viewport.
Re-open M7-CONTENT-LOCK-ACCEPTANCE-001 only after QA accepts this validation task.

decisions.decision: This task validates UX recovery only; it must not approve formal content lock.
Manual node battle remains DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE and is out of scope.
Evidence must focus on player-mode comprehension, default map semantics, task rail visibility, and absence of debug labels.

risks.summary: Product owner previously rejected content lock due player-facing UX confusion; tests must catch understandable first-screen failures rather than only code health.

requested_action: Implement or update UX smoke tests and screenshot evidence within allowed paths, run required tests, then write the writer REVIEW handoff.

tests_run.result: Clean main at ade15c64 before creating codex/m7-ux-recovery-validation.
M7-UX-RECOVERY-VALIDATION-001 was the only READY task before start.
